steps.instruction_text: Let's start by navigating to the CRM module where you can manage your activities.
Click on a record to open its details and access the chatter section.
In the chatter section, click the 'Activity' button to schedule a new activity.
In the Schedule Activity pop-up, select the type of activity you want to schedule, like a 'Call' or 'Meeting'.
Enter a summary for your activity, such as 'Follow up on proposal'.
Set a due date for the activity using the calendar popover.
Assign the activity to yourself or another user from the drop-down menu.
Add any additional notes or information in the 'Log a note' field.
Click the 'Save' button to schedule the activity and add it to the planned activities list.
Observe how the activity is now listed under 'Planned Activities' in the chatter.
Let's start by navigating to the Contacts app. Click on the Contacts app to open it.
To create a new contact, click the New button.
Select whether this contact is a Person or a Company. This is a mandatory field.
Enter the name of the Person or Company. This is how the name will appear throughout the database.
Fill in the email address in the envelope field and the phone number with the appropriate country code in the phone field.
Now, enter the address of the Company or Person.
If applicable, enter additional details such as Job Position, Tax ID, Website, and Language.
To add multiple addresses, go to the Contacts tab and click Add Contact.
In the Create Contact pop-up, select the type of address you want to add, such as Contact, Invoice, or Delivery.
Enter the contact information for this address type, including Contact Name, Address, Email, and Phone.
Once all information is entered, click Save & Close to save the contact details.
Navigate to the Sales app to start exploring search and filter options.
Click on the Reporting menu to access the Sales Analysis report.
Observe the search bar at the top of the view. This is where you can search, filter, and group records.
Type 'Mitch' in the search bar to find records related to this salesperson.
Click the dropdown icon to access the Filters menu.
Select 'Quotations' from the Filters menu to include records at the quotation stage.
Remove the 'Order Date: Last 365 Days' filter by clicking the cancel icon next to it.
Add a custom filter by selecting 'Add Custom Filter' from the Filters menu.
In the Add Custom Filter window, set up a filter to match sales from the year 2024.
Click the Apply button to see the filtered results.
Navigate to the app where you want to view reports, such as Sales or Inventory.
Click on the Reporting menu to access the available reports.
Observe the different report views available. You can choose between Graph and Pivot views.
Click the graph view button at the top right to visualize data patterns and trends.
Now, click the pivot view button at the top right to aggregate and analyze your data.
Click on Measures to choose what data to measure. You can select multiple measures in the pivot view.
Use the Group By option to organize your data by different dimensions, like Product Category.
Try adding a group by clicking the plus button next to the Total header in the pivot table.
Switch to the graph view and choose between bar, line, or pie charts to display your data.
For bar and line charts, try using the stacked option to see how data groups appear on top of each other.
Let's start by exporting some data. Navigate to the list view of the records you want to export.
Select the records you wish to export by ticking the checkboxes next to them.
Click the Action button and choose Export to start the export process.
In the Export Data window, choose the fields you want to export. Use the Search bar to find specific fields.
Select your preferred export format, either CSV or XLS, and click the Export button.
Now, let's import some data. Navigate to the object view where you want to import data.
Click the Action button and select Import records from the dropdown menu.
Upload your CSV or XLSX file containing the data you want to import.
Map the columns in your file to the corresponding fields in Odoo, then click the Import button to complete the process.
Navigate to the module where you want to edit text, such as Internal Notes or Description.
Click on the field you want to edit to activate the rich-text editor.
Double-click a word or sentence to display the text editor toolbar.
Use the toolbar to apply formatting like bold, italics, or underline to your text.
Click the 'Expand toolbar' icon for more options like font family, text alignment, and list formatting.
Type '/' to open the powerbox and explore commands for inserting tables, images, or lists.
Hover over any text element to reveal the drag icon, allowing you to reposition elements.
Experiment with the Odoo AI feature by writing a prompt to generate content or translations.
Use keyboard shortcuts like CTRL/CMD + B for bold or CTRL/CMD + U for underline to quickly format text.
Let's explore In-App Purchases (IAP) in Odoo. Start by navigating to the Invoicing app.
Click on the 'Create' button to start a new invoice and see how IAP services can be integrated.
Observe the available IAP services that can enhance your Odoo experience, such as SMS and Documents Digitization.
Navigate to the Contacts app to see how the SMS IAP service works.
Click on a contact with a mobile number to send an SMS using the IAP service.
Find and click the SMS icon next to the contact's phone number to open the SMS sending window.
Type your message in the 'Message' field and click 'Send SMS' to send it.
After sending, observe how credits are deducted from your prepaid balance.
If you need more credits, navigate to the Settings app to purchase additional credits.
In the Settings app, go to the Contacts section to manage your IAP credits.
Let's start by exploring the keyboard shortcuts in Odoo. Hold down the Ctrl key to view the shortcuts available on your screen.
To quickly navigate to the Odoo Home Page, press Alt + H on Windows/Linux or Ctrl + H on macOS.
Try creating a new record by pressing Alt + C on Windows/Linux or Ctrl + C on macOS.
If you want to discard changes, press Alt + J on Windows/Linux or Ctrl + J on macOS.
To save any changes you've made, press Alt + S on Windows/Linux or Ctrl + S on macOS.
Need to search for something? Use Alt + Q on Windows/Linux or Ctrl + Q on macOS to open the search bar.
Open the command palette by pressing Ctrl + K on Windows/Linux or Command + K on macOS. This is a powerful tool for quickly finding menus and modules.
Once the command palette is open, try searching for a menu by typing '/' and then the menu name.
Finally, to open a selected item in a new tab, press Ctrl + Enter.
Navigate to the Odoo Studio to start customizing your form view.
Click on the gear icon to access the Actions menu.
Select 'Edit Properties' to begin adding a new property field.
In the popover, type a label for your new property field.
Choose a field type from the dropdown menu to define the kind of data this property will hold.
If applicable, enter a default value for your new property field.
Click the 'Add a Property' button to include another property field if needed.
Observe how the property fields are displayed in the form view for all records linked to the same parent.
To edit an existing property, hover over it and click the pencil icon.
Make any necessary changes in the popover and click outside to save.
First, let's activate developer mode so we can access the stages menu. Navigate to the Settings app.
In the Settings app, go to General Settings and click on Activate the developer mode under Developer Tools.
Now, let's head over to the Helpdesk app to view and modify stages. Click on the Helpdesk app.
Go to Configuration and select Stages to see the current list of stages.
To create a new stage, click the New button at the top-left of the stage list.
Enter a name for your new stage in the Name field.
To enable the Days to rot feature, click on the Settings icon for the relevant stage and select Edit.
Enter the number of days in the Days to rot field to set the threshold before action is needed.
Click Save to apply the configuration for the Days to rot feature.
To fold a stage, tick the Folded in Kanban checkbox on the Stages form.
Navigate to the Settings app to manage users.
In the Users section, click on the New button to add a new user.
Fill in the form with the new user's information, including their email address.
Navigate to the Access Rights tab to assign the appropriate user group.
After filling out all necessary fields, click the Save button to create the user.
Observe that an invitation email is automatically sent to the new user.
To check logged-in devices, click on your user icon in the upper-right corner and select My Profile.
In the Devices tab, observe the list of devices that have accessed the database.
If you need to revoke access for a device, click on the Revoke button.
Enter your password in the Security Control pop-up to confirm revoking access.
To deactivate a user, select the user from the Manage Users list and choose Archive from the Actions menu.
Confirm the deactivation in the pop-up window by clicking OK.
Let's start by navigating to the Settings app to manage companies.
In the Settings app, go to the Companies section and click on 'Manage Companies'.
To create a new company, click the 'New' button.
Fill in the company details such as Company Name, Address, and Tax ID.
Don't forget to upload the company's logo for a complete setup.
Once all details are filled, click the 'Save' button to create the company.
Now, let's add users to the company. Navigate to the Users & Companies section.
Click on 'Add a User' to start adding a new user to the company.
Enter the user's details and configure their access rights appropriately.
Finally, save the user details to complete the setup.
Navigate to the Apps dashboard to manage your Odoo apps and modules.
Click on the search bar to find the app you want to install, or scroll through the list.
To install an app, click the Activate button on the app's card.
To upgrade an app, click the vertical ellipsis icon on the app's card and select Upgrade.
To uninstall an app, click the vertical ellipsis icon on the app's card and select Uninstall.
Observe the Apps to Uninstall section to see which applications will be removed.
Check the Show All checkbox to display all module dependencies before proceeding.
To finalize the uninstallation, click the Uninstall button.
Let's start by navigating to the Settings app to activate Developer Mode.
Scroll down to find the Developer Tools section.
Click the 'Activate the developer mode' button to enable Developer Mode.
Notice that you now have the option to 'Deactivate the developer mode'.
To access developer tools, look for the bug icon in the top navigation bar.
Click the bug icon to explore the developer tools available.
Explore the technical menu in the Settings app for advanced options.
Remember, you can activate Developer Mode from any screen by adding '?debug=1' to the URL.
Try using the command palette (Ctrl + K or Cmd ⌘ + K) to quickly toggle Developer Mode.
Let's start by navigating to the CRM module where you can manage your opportunities.
Click on an opportunity to open its detailed view and see the chatter on the right side.
Observe the chatter section where you can send emails, log notes, and schedule activities.
Click the 'Send Message' button to compose a new email to the followers of this opportunity.
Type your message in the provided field and then click 'Send' to dispatch your email.
Now, let's explore how to configure your email domain. Navigate to the Settings module.
In the settings, find the Email section to configure your custom domain for outgoing emails.
If you want to use your own domain, additional configuration is necessary. Consult your domain provider's documentation for DNS and mail protocol settings.
Remember, if you prefer using Odoo's default email settings, no additional configuration is required.
Let's explore how to integrate Odoo with external services. Start by navigating to the Settings module.
In the Settings, look for the Integrations section to see available options.
Click on the Mail Plugins option to learn how to connect your email services with Odoo.
Next, let's check out the Unsplash integration for adding high-quality images. Click on the Unsplash option.
Observe how you can use geolocation services to enhance your Odoo experience. Click on the Geolocation option.
Explore the Google Translate integration to enable multilingual support. Click on the Google Translate option.
Finally, discover how to connect to cloud storage services. Click on the Cloud Storage option.
Welcome to Odoo's Internet of Things (IoT) module! Let's start by navigating to the IoT section.
Click on the 'Create' button to set up a new IoT box.
Type the name of your IoT box in the 'Name' field.
Enter the serial number of your IoT box in the 'Description' field.
Click the 'Save' button to save your new IoT box configuration.
Now, let's connect a device to your IoT system. Click on the 'Devices' tab.
Observe the list of compatible devices you can connect to your IoT system.
Select a device from the list and click 'Connect' to link it with your IoT system.
Finally, ensure your IoT box subscription is active for production use.
Navigate to the Studio app to begin creating a new model.
Click the Create button to start a new model.
Type the name of your new model in the Name field.
Observe the suggested features that you can add to your model to enhance its functionality.
Select the Contact details feature to add a Many2One field linked to the Contact model.
Choose the User assignment feature to add a Responsible field with user avatars.
Add the Date & Calendar feature to include a Date field and activate the Calendar view.
Click the Save button to finalize your new model with the selected features.
Navigate to the Kanban view to see how the Picture and Pipeline stages features enhance the layout.
Observe how the selected features are integrated into your model, providing a comprehensive view.
Let's explore how fields structure the models in Odoo. Navigate to the Studio module to get started.
Click on the 'Create' button to add a new field to a model.
Select 'Text' as the field type to add a simple text field to your model.
Observe how you can choose different widgets like 'Badge' or 'E-mail' for the Text field.
Now, let's add a 'Multiline Text' field for longer text entries. Select 'Multiline Text' from the field type options.
Notice the 'Copy to Clipboard' widget option available for Multiline Text fields.
Next, let's add an 'Integer' field. Click on 'Integer' from the field type options.
Check out the widget options like 'Percentage Pie' and 'Progress Bar' for Integer fields.
Finally, let's add a 'Monetary' field. Select 'Monetary' from the field type options.
Notice how Odoo prompts you to add a 'Currency' field if it doesn't already exist.
Let's start by accessing the Studio app. Click on the Studio icon to open it.
Now that you're in Studio, navigate to the Views section to see the different view types available.
Click on the ellipsis icon next to a view to explore more options, such as setting it as the default view.
To modify a view, activate Developer mode and select the View tab to edit using the XML editor.
Let's add a new button to a Form view. Click on 'Add a button' at the top of the form.
Enter a label for your new button and choose the action it should perform.
To make your button stand out, change its style to a primary button in the Properties tab.
Now, let's add a smart button. Click the plus icon in the top-right corner of the form.
Enter a label and choose an icon for your smart button, then select a related field.
Finally, let's add a custom filter to a Search view. Drag and drop a filter under the Filters section.
Navigate to the Studio app by clicking on the Studio icon.
Click on 'Automations' to start creating a new automation rule.
Click the 'New' button to create a new automation rule.
Type a clear and meaningful name for your automation rule in the provided field.
Select a trigger for your automation rule. This defines the event that will activate your rule.
If necessary, fill in any additional fields that appear based on your chosen trigger.
Click 'Add an action' under the 'Actions To Do' tab to define what the automation rule should do.
Choose the type of action you want to perform and complete the relevant fields.
Click 'Save & Close' to finish creating your automation rule, or 'Save & New' to add more actions.
Navigate to the Settings module to begin customizing your PDF report layout.
In the Companies section, click on Configure Document Layout to access layout settings.
Observe the different layout options available for your reports, such as Light, Boxed, and Bold.
Click the Edit button to change the company logo that appears on your reports.
Type in the new tagline for your company to be displayed on the report header or footer.
Select your preferred paper format for the reports, such as A4 or US Letter.
Now, let's create a new PDF report. Click the Toggle Studio button in the model you want to create a report for.
Click on Reports, then click New to start creating a new report.
Choose the type of report you want to create: External, Internal, or Blank.
Observe the header and footer settings for the report type you selected.
Click Save to finalize your new PDF report setup.
Let's start by opening the Studio app to configure approval rules.
Select the button you want to apply the approval rule to.
In the Properties tab, click 'Add an approval step' to begin setting up the rule.
Specify the users responsible for approving the action by filling in the 'Approvers' or 'Approver Group' fields.
Optionally, select users to notify when the action is approved or rejected.
Add a description to be displayed on the button if needed.
Click 'Add an approval step' again if you need multiple steps, and adjust the approval order as needed.
Observe how the user avatar icon appears next to the button label, indicating the approval steps.
Click the user avatar icon to approve or reject the action.
Remember, you can delegate approval rights to other users if needed.
Welcome to Odoo Invoicing! Let's start by navigating to the Invoicing app to create a new invoice.
Click the Create button to start a new invoice.
Type the customer's name in the Customer field to select who this invoice is for.
Enter the product details in the Product field to specify what the invoice is for.
Type the amount in the Amount field to set the total for this invoice.
Click the Save button to save your new invoice.
Observe how Odoo automatically creates the necessary journal entries for your invoice using double-entry bookkeeping.
Now, let's navigate to the Reporting section to view financial reports.
Observe the various financial reports available, such as profit and loss statements and balance sheets.
Let's start by navigating to the Expenses application. Click on the Expenses app to open it.
To create a new expense, click the Create button.
Enter the name of the expense in the Name field.
Specify the amount of the expense in the Amount field.
Select the date of the expense in the Date field.
Choose the type of expense from the Type dropdown.
Once all details are filled in, click the Save button to save your expense.
Now, let's submit the expense for approval by clicking the Submit button.
Observe the status bar to see the current status of your expense submission.
Navigate to the Invoicing app to start setting up payment providers.
Click on Configuration in the menu, then select Payment Providers to view the list of available providers.
Observe the list of supported payment providers. These allow your customers to pay online using various methods.
Click on a payment provider card, such as Stripe, to access its configuration settings.
Enter the necessary credentials and settings for the payment provider to enable it.
Click the Save button to apply your configuration changes.
Navigate to the Website app to configure payment providers for your eCommerce platform.
Go to Configuration and select Payment Providers to manage online payment options for your website.
Observe the payment provider options available for your eCommerce site. These ensure seamless transactions for your customers.
Let's start by navigating to the Invoicing app to check your fiscal localization settings.
Click on the Configuration menu, then select Settings to access the fiscal localization options.
Scroll down to the Fiscal Localization section and observe the Package field.
If you need to change the fiscal localization package, click on the dropdown menu in the Package field.
Select the appropriate package for your company's country from the list. Remember, you can only change this if no entries have been posted.
Once you've selected the correct package, click the Save button to apply your changes.
Now, let's verify the list of countries that have available fiscal localization modules. Navigate to the Settings app.
In the Settings app, look for the Fiscal Localizations section to see the supported countries.
Navigate to the ESG app to begin managing your sustainability data.
Click on 'Collect' under the ESG menu to view your emissions data.
Click 'New' to manually enter emissions data for activities not tracked automatically.
Enter the name of the activity, select a date, and choose an emission factor.
Input the quantity of emissions for the activity and click 'Save' to record the data.
Navigate to 'Configuration' and select 'Source Databases' to import emission factors.
Click 'Download' next to the ADEME database to import certified emission factors.
Observe how the imported emission factors are now available for use in your reports.
Go to 'Configuration' and select 'Gases' to add more specific greenhouse gases if needed.
Click 'Add' to include a new gas type, ensuring detailed breakdowns in your emissions data.
Welcome to Odoo CRM! Let's start by navigating to the CRM module to manage your sales activities.
Click on the 'Create' button to start a new opportunity in your sales pipeline.
Enter the name of your opportunity in the 'Opportunity' field.
Select a customer for this opportunity by typing in the 'Customer' field.
Now, let's set a deadline for this opportunity by choosing a date in the 'Expected Closing' field.
Click the 'Save' button to add this opportunity to your pipeline.
Observe the pipeline view where your new opportunity is now listed. This helps you track and manage your sales process.
Click on a kanban card to view details and update the status of an opportunity.
Schedule a follow-up meeting by clicking on the 'Schedule Activity' button.
Fill in the details for your meeting and click 'Save' to schedule it.
Finally, observe how your scheduled activities appear in the opportunity's timeline, helping you stay organized.
Navigate to the Sales application to begin managing your sales process.
Click the Create button to start a new sales quotation.
Type the customer's name in the Customer field to associate this quotation with a client.
Add a product to the quotation by typing its name in the Product field.
Enter the quantity of the product the customer wishes to purchase.
Click the Save button to save your quotation.
Observe the status bar to see the current status of your quotation.
Click the Confirm button to convert the quotation into a sales order.
Navigate to the Invoicing section to create an invoice for the sales order.
Click the Create button to generate a new invoice for the sales order.
Navigate to the Point of Sale module to get started with managing your shop or restaurant.
Click the Create button to set up a new Point of Sale session.
Type in the name of your Point of Sale session in the Name field.
Select the type of Point of Sale setup you need: Store, Restaurant, or Self-ordering kiosk.
Observe how you can integrate various hardware like payment terminals and barcode scanners.
Click the Save button to save your Point of Sale configuration.
Navigate to the Products section to manage the items you will sell through your Point of Sale.
Click the New button to add a new product to your Point of Sale catalog.
Type in the product details such as name, price, and category.
Click the Save button to add the product to your catalog.
Navigate to the Subscriptions app to begin managing your subscription plans.
Click on the Configuration menu to access recurring plans.
Select Recurring Plans to view existing plans or create a new one.
Click the New button to create a new recurring plan.
In the Name field, type a suitable name for your plan, like 'Monthly' or 'Quarterly'.
Set the Billing Period by choosing the unit of time and entering the numerical value.
Enter the number of days for Automatic Closing if payment is not made.
Decide if you want to Align to Period Start for billing on the first day of the next period.
Optionally, assign an Invoice Email Template to enhance communication with customers.
Click the Save button to finalize your new recurring plan.
Navigate to the Rental app by clicking on the Rental icon from the home screen.
Observe the Rental Orders dashboard. Here, you can see all current rentals, their status, and related details.
Click on the Configuration menu to access the Settings page for rental configurations.
In the Settings, explore options to configure Default Delay Costs and Default Padding Time for rentals.
Navigate to the Products page to view all available rental products.
Click on a product to view or edit its rental pricing details.
In the Rental prices tab, click 'Add a price' to set a custom rental price and period.
Type the desired price and select the rental period from the options available.
Finally, click the Save button to apply your changes to the rental product.
Welcome to Odoo Website Builder! Let's start by navigating to the Website app to begin creating your site.
Click on the 'Create' button to start designing a new webpage using Odoo's building blocks.
Type the name of your new webpage in the 'Name' field to give it a title.
Customize your page layout by dragging and dropping building blocks from the sidebar.
Add multimedia content like images or videos to make your page more engaging.
Navigate to the 'Structure' section to manage your website's pages and menus.
Click on the 'Configuration' card to set up your domain name and other important settings.
Observe how you can enhance your website's performance by configuring a Content Delivery Network (CDN).
Finally, check the 'Reporting' section to monitor your website's traffic and set up link trackers.
Navigate to the eCommerce module to start setting up your online store.
Click on the 'Create' button to add a new product to your store.
Type the product name in the provided field.
Enter the product price in the 'Amount' field.
Save your new product by clicking the 'Save' button.
Observe the product list to ensure your new product appears correctly.
Navigate to the 'Design' section to customize your product catalog.
Use the available design elements to enhance the look of your product pages.
Click on 'Configure Delivery Methods' to set up shipping options for your store.
Select the delivery methods you want to offer to your customers.
Finally, navigate to the 'Order Handling' section to manage your e-commerce orders.
Review the order lifecycle options, including sales, stock management, and returns.
Navigate to the Apps application to begin installing Live Chat.
In the Apps search bar, type 'Live Chat' and click the Install button.
Once installed, navigate to the Live Chat app to create a new chat channel.
Click the New button to start creating a new live chat channel.
Enter a name for your new channel in the Channel Name field.
Click on the Operators tab to manage who can respond to chats.
Add operators by clicking the Add button and selecting users from the list.
Navigate to the Options tab to customize the appearance of the live chat window.
Update the Notification text to personalize the greeting for visitors.
Change the Livechat Button Color to match your website's theme.
Click Save to apply all your changes and activate your live chat channel.
Navigate to the eLearning module to start managing your courses.
Click on the 'Courses' section to view all available courses.
To create a new course, click the 'New' button.
Enter a title for your course in the 'Course Title' field.
Add tags to categorize your course by typing in the 'Tags' field.
Hover over the camera icon and click 'Edit' to add an image for your course.
Switch to the 'Content' tab to start adding sections and content to your course.
Click 'Add Section' to organize your course into different parts.
In the 'Options' tab, assign a responsible user for the course.
Define who can see the course by setting the 'Show course to' option.
Finally, click the 'Save' button to save your new course.
Navigate to the Website module to start setting up your forum.
Go to Configuration and select Forum: Forums to manage your forums.
Click the New button to create a new forum.
Enter a name for your forum in the Forum Name field.
Choose the forum mode: select Questions to allow marking answers as best, or Discussions for open dialogue.
Set the Default Sort option to determine how questions will be sorted by default.
Select the Privacy setting for your forum to control who can view it.
Navigate to the Karma Gains tab to configure how users earn karma points.
Adjust the karma points for different interactions by selecting and editing the values.
Go to the Karma Related Rights tab to set the karma points required for different forum functionalities.
Edit the karma requirements for functionalities like asking questions or moderating posts.
Click the Save button to finalize your forum setup.
Navigate to the Website module to start managing your blog.
Click on Configuration and then Blogs to view your existing blogs or create a new one.
Click the New button to create a new blog. Enter the Blog Name and Blog Subtitle.
Now, let's add a blog post. Click +New in the top-right corner and select Blog Post.
In the pop-up, select the blog where the post should appear, write the post's Title, and click Save.
Write your blog post content using the website builder. Remember, you can type '/' to format and add elements.
Don't forget to toggle the Unpublished switch in the top-right corner to publish your post.
To add tags, click Edit, then Customize, and choose a record under Tags.
Customize your blog homepage by clicking Edit and then Customize. Adjust the layout and sidebar as needed.
Finally, customize individual blog posts by opening a post and clicking Edit, then Customize.
Let's start by navigating to the Inventory app. Click on the Inventory icon to open it.
Welcome to the Inventory dashboard! Here, you can see an overview of your inventory operations.
To manage products, click on the 'Products' menu item.
Let's add a new product. Click the Create button to start.
Type the product name in the Name field.
Enter the product's cost in the Cost field.
Now, let's save the product. Click the Save button.
Great! You've successfully added a new product. Next, let's explore warehouse management.
Click on the 'Warehouses' menu to view and manage your warehouses.
Here you can see all your warehouses and their details. Feel free to explore!
Navigate to the Purchase module to begin managing your purchase orders.
Click the Create button to start a new purchase order.
Enter the vendor's name in the Vendor field to specify who you are purchasing from.
Add the products you wish to purchase by typing in the Product field.
Specify the quantity and price for each product in the respective fields.
Click the Save button to store your purchase order details.
Observe the status bar to check the current status of your purchase order.
Click the Confirm button to confirm your purchase order and proceed with the procurement.
Monitor your purchase orders by viewing the list of all orders in the Purchase module.
Navigate to the Manufacturing module to start managing your production processes.
Click the Create button to start a new manufacturing order.
Type the name of the product you want to manufacture in the Product field.
Enter the quantity you wish to produce in the Quantity field.
Select the scheduled date for the manufacturing order in the Date field.
Observe the work center control panel to monitor the status of your work orders.
Click the Save button to save your manufacturing order.
Once your order is ready, click the Confirm button to initiate the manufacturing process.
Use the tablets on the shop floor to control work orders in real-time.
If any issues arise, trigger maintenance operations or quality checks directly from the control panel.
Navigate to the Inventory module to start managing products with barcodes.
Click the 'Products' menu to view your list of products.
Select a product from the list to assign a barcode.
Click the 'Edit' button to modify the product details.
Type the barcode number into the 'Barcode' field.
Click the 'Save' button to store the barcode information.
Navigate to the 'Operations' menu to manage inventory movements using barcodes.
Observe how scanning a barcode with a connected scanner can trigger inventory processes.
Navigate to the Quality module to start managing product quality.
Click on the 'Quality' tab to access quality management features.
Click the 'Create' button to set up a new quality check.
Type the name of the quality check in the 'Name' field.
Select the type of quality check from the 'Type' dropdown menu.
Observe the options available for automating quality inspection frequency.
Click the 'Save' button to save your new quality check.
Navigate to the 'Quality Alerts' section to manage alerts for quality issues.
Click the 'Create' button to report a new quality alert.
Fill in the details of the quality issue in the 'Description' field.
Click the 'Submit' button to submit the quality alert.
Observe how the quality alert is now listed in the system for further action.
Let's start by navigating to the Maintenance module to manage your equipment's upkeep.
Click the Create button to add a new piece of equipment that needs maintenance tracking.
Type in the name of the equipment in the Name field.
Enter the maintenance type, such as preventive or corrective, in the Type field.
Now, let's save the equipment details by clicking the Save button.
Observe the list of equipment to ensure your new entry appears correctly.
Navigate to the Maintenance Requests to view and manage any ongoing maintenance tasks.
Click on a maintenance request to view its details and status.
If the maintenance task is complete, click the Confirm button to update its status.
Navigate to the Repairs module to start managing repair orders.
Click the Create button to start a new repair order.
Enter the name of the product that needs repair in the Product field.
Specify the customer returning the product in the Customer field.
Set the expected repair date in the Date field.
Add any additional details about the repair in the Description field.
Click the Save button to save your new repair order.
Observe the status bar to ensure the repair order is correctly recorded.
Welcome to Odoo's Product Lifecycle Management! Let's start by navigating to the PLM module.
Click on the 'Create' button to start a new product lifecycle management process.
Type the name of the product you are managing in the 'Name' field.
Enter the initial details of your product, such as description and type, to define its basic attributes.
Observe how you can add stakeholders to review and approve changes before implementation.
Click the 'Save' button to store your product's initial lifecycle data.
Navigate to the 'Manage Changes' section to handle product iterations and improvements.
Click on a change request to see its details and the approval process.
Observe how the change request includes options for collaboration and feedback.
Finally, click the 'Approve' button to approve a change request and move forward in the lifecycle.
Let's start by navigating to the Employees app to manage your personnel files.
Click the Create button to set up a new employee record.
Type the new employee's name in the Name field.
Now, let's assign a department to the employee by selecting from the list.
Observe how you can manage employee certifications and training from this screen.
Navigate to the Configuration section to adjust settings for employee presence display.
Enable the Skills Management option to display the resume tab on employee profiles.
Finally, set the default Company Working Hours using the drop-down menu.
Navigate to the Payroll app to begin setting up your payroll system.
Click on the Configuration menu and select Settings to configure your payroll settings.
Enable the Payroll Entries option to post payroll slips in the Accounting app.
Enable the Payroll SEPA option if you need to create Single Euro Payments Area payments.
Choose a Payroll Localization to ensure compliance with local laws and regulations.
Click Install on the desired localization to set it up for your payroll system.
Navigate to the Employees app to ensure all employee contracts are properly configured.
Check that each employee has a signed contract outlining their compensation and benefits.
Return to the Payroll app to process payslips once all contracts and settings are verified.
Click on the Payslips menu to create and manage employee payslips.
Resolve any errors with work entries before processing payroll to ensure accuracy.
Once all issues are resolved, process the payslips to complete the payroll cycle.
Navigate to the Time Off app to start managing your time off settings.
Click on the Configuration menu to view and manage Time Off Types.
Observe the list of preconfigured time off types such as Paid Time Off and Sick Time Off.
Click the New button to create a new time off type.
Type the name of the new time off type, like 'Vacation', in the Name field.
Select the approval method required for time off requests, such as 'By Time Off Officer'.
Choose whether this time off type requires allocation by selecting 'Yes' or 'No Limit'.
Decide if employees can request extra days and select the appropriate option.
Click the Save button to finalize the creation of your new time off type.
Let's start by navigating to the Recruitment app to manage job applicants.
Click on the 'Create' button to add a new job position.
Type the name of the job position in the 'Job Title' field.
Observe how an applicant card is automatically created when an applicant applies for a job.
Click on an applicant card to view their details.
Enable the 'Send Interview Survey' option to automatically send surveys to applicants.
Click the 'Save' button to apply the changes you've made in the settings.
Enable 'Résumé Display' to show the applicant's résumé on their card by default.
Observe how the résumé appears on the right side of the applicant's card when enabled.
Enable 'Résumé Digitization (OCR)' to automatically extract information from résumés.
Select the 'Digitize on demand' option to control when résumés are digitized.
Finally, click the 'Save' button to ensure all your settings are applied.
Navigate to the Employees app to start managing appraisals.
Click on the Appraisals menu to view all appraisal-related options.
Click the Create button to schedule a new appraisal.
Type the name of the employee you want to appraise in the Name field.
Select the appraisal date by clicking on the Date field.
Choose an appraisal template to use for this session.
Click the Save button to finalize the appraisal setup.
Observe the list of scheduled appraisals to ensure your new entry appears.
Welcome to the Attendances app! Let's start by navigating to the Attendances application from the home screen.
Click on the 'Configuration' menu to access the settings for the Attendances app.
Observe the settings available for configuring how employees check in and out, and how extra hours are computed.
Now, let's check the access rights for a user. Navigate to the Settings app.
Click on 'Users & Companies' and then 'Users' to view user access rights.
Select a user to view their access rights. Scroll down to the Human Resources section to see their Attendances access level.
Let's check the Attendance approver for an employee. Navigate to the Employees app.
Click on an employee's record to view their details.
Go to the Settings tab and scroll to the Approvers section to see who can manage this employee's attendance records.
Finally, let's explore how to activate the 'Automatic Check-Out' feature in the Attendances app settings.
In the settings, find the 'Automatic Check-Out' option and note the Tolerance field that appears when it's activated.
Navigate to the Referrals app by clicking on the app icon.
Observe the onboarding slide with the message 'OH NO! VILLAINS ARE LURKING THE CITY! HELP US RECRUIT A TEAM OF SUPERHEROES TO SAVE THE DAY!'
Click the 'Next' button to proceed to the next onboarding slide.
Read the next slide: 'BROWSE THROUGH OPEN JOB POSITIONS, PROMOTE THEM ON SOCIAL MEDIA, OR REFER FRIENDS.'
Continue to the next slide by clicking 'Next'.
On the third slide, observe the message: 'COLLECT POINTS AND EXCHANGE THEM FOR AWESOME GIFTS IN THE SHOP.'
Click 'Next' to view the final onboarding slide.
Read the final slide: 'COMPETE AGAINST YOUR COLLEAGUES TO BUILD THE BEST JUSTICE LEAGUE!'
Click the 'Start Now' button to complete the onboarding process and access the main dashboard.
On the main dashboard, observe the points you've earned and the rewards available.
Welcome to the Fleet Management module! Let's start by navigating to the Fleet app to manage your vehicles.
Click the 'Create' button to add a new vehicle to your fleet.
Enter the vehicle's name in the 'Name' field to identify it easily.
Fill in the purchase date of the vehicle in the 'Date' field.
Now, let's specify the cost of the vehicle in the 'Amount' field.
Click the 'Save' button to store the vehicle information.
Observe the list of vehicles in your fleet. You can see the newly added vehicle here.
To manage vehicle services, click on a vehicle's row to view its details.
Navigate to the 'Services' tab to record and track maintenance activities.
Click the 'Create' button to add a new service record for the vehicle.
Enter the service details, including the type and cost, to keep track of maintenance expenses.
Finally, click 'Save' to record the service details. Great job managing your fleet!
Navigate to the Frontdesk app by clicking on the app icon.
Let's configure a new station. Go to Configuration and select Stations.
Click the New button to create a new station.
Enter a name for the station in the Frontdesk Name field, such as 'Main Lobby'.
Select the responsible person(s) who will be alerted when a visitor checks in.
Click the Save button to generate the Kiosk URL for this station.
Observe the Kiosk URL field. This URL is used to access the station's sign-in page.
Enable the Host Selection option to allow visitors to choose their meeting host.
Select the Theme for the kiosk, choosing between Light or Dark mode.
Enable the Self Check-In option to allow guests to use a QR code for check-in.
If desired, enable the Offer Drinks option to provide beverage choices to visitors.
Navigate to the Lunch app by clicking on the app-expenses icon.
Let's start by configuring the settings. Click on Configuration and then Settings.
In the Settings menu, enter the maximum overdraft amount for employees in the Lunch Overdraft field.
Now, set the Reception notification message that users will receive when their food is delivered.
If you have multiple languages, click on the language code next to the Reception notification to add translations.
Navigate to Locations under Configuration to manage where food can be delivered.
Click the New button to add a new location for food delivery.
Enter the name of the new location and its address in the respective fields.
Now, let's set up an alert. Go to Alerts under Configuration.
Click the New button to create a new alert for the Lunch app.
Fill in the Alert Name and Message fields, and choose how the alert should be displayed.
Finally, click the Save button to save your new alert configuration.
Welcome to the Email Marketing app! Let's start by navigating to the Email Marketing module.
Here we are on the Mailings dashboard. Observe the default list view showing your email campaigns.
Let's switch to the Kanban view to see your campaigns in different stages. Click the Kanban view icon.
In the Kanban view, you can see your emails in stages like Draft, In Queue, and Sent. Drag an email card to move it to a different stage.
Now, let's view your campaigns on a calendar. Click the Calendar view icon.
In the Calendar view, you can see when emails are scheduled or have been sent. Notice the current date marked with a red circle.
Let's go back to the List view to explore more options. Click the List view icon.
To customize the columns, click the Additional Options icon on the far-right of the column titles.
Great job! You've learned how to navigate through different views in the Email Marketing app. Feel free to explore more!
Navigate to the SMS Marketing application by clicking on the app icon.
Observe the SMS Marketing dashboard, showcasing your SMS campaigns in a Kanban view.
Click the 'Create' button to start a new SMS campaign.
Type the name of your SMS campaign in the 'Name' field.
Enter the message content in the 'Description' field.
Select the recipients for your SMS campaign by choosing a mailing list.
Click the 'Save' button to save your SMS campaign details.
Switch to the 'Calendar' view to schedule your SMS campaign.
Click on a future date to schedule your SMS campaign for that day.
Finally, click the 'Send' button to queue your SMS campaign for delivery.
Navigate to the Social Marketing app to start managing your social media accounts.
Click the 'Add A Stream' button to link a new social media account.
In the pop-up window, select 'Link a new account' to choose a social media platform.
Choose a social media platform like Facebook or Instagram to link your business account.
Observe how Odoo redirects you to the social media platform's authorization page.
Once authorization is granted, Odoo will return to the Social Marketing dashboard.
Notice the new column added to your dashboard with posts from the linked account.
Navigate to Configuration and select 'Social Media' to view all available platforms.
Click 'Link account' next to a platform to quickly connect another social media account.
Finally, navigate to 'Social Accounts' under Configuration to see all linked accounts.
Let's start by navigating to the Events module to explore the dashboard.
Observe the Events dashboard displayed in Kanban view, showcasing various event stages.
Click the plus icon at the top of a stage to add a new event.
In the blank Kanban card, type the name of the event and set the start and end dates.
Click Add to save the event to the stage, or Edit to configure more details.
Now, let's explore the settings. Navigate to Configuration and then Settings.
In the Events section, observe the various features you can enable, like Schedule & Tracks.
Enable the Schedule & Tracks feature to manage event schedules and tracks.
Don't forget to click Save to apply the changes to your event settings.
Navigate to the Marketing Automation application to begin creating a new campaign.
Click the New button to start creating a new marketing campaign.
Type a name for your campaign in the Name field to identify it easily.
Select a target audience by configuring the Target and Filter section.
Define the activities for your campaign by adding workflow activities.
Click the Save button to save your new campaign setup.
To test your campaign, click on the Tests smart button at the top of the campaign form.
Review the number of participants engaged in the test run from the Tests smart button.
Once satisfied with the test results, click the Confirm button to launch your campaign.
Navigate to the Surveys application by clicking on the Surveys icon from the dashboard.
Click the New button to start creating a new survey.
Type the name of your new survey in the Name field.
Observe the survey dashboard to see all the surveys listed in a Kanban view.
Click on a survey card to view its details and statistics.
Click the Share button to invite participants to your survey.
Toggle the Send by Email switch to send survey invitations via email.
Click the Test button to preview the survey as a participant.
Click the See results button to analyze the survey responses and metrics.
Click the Start Live Session button to initiate a live survey session.
Let's start by navigating to the Project module to manage your ongoing projects.
Click the Create button to start a new project.
Enter the name of your new project in the Name field.
Now, let's add a task to your project. Click on the Tasks tab.
Click the Create button to add a new task to your project.
Type the task name in the Name field to specify what needs to be done.
Assign the task to a coworker by selecting their name from the Assignee dropdown.
Click the Save button to save your task.
Observe the list of tasks to ensure your new task appears correctly.
Finally, navigate back to the main project dashboard to view the overall status of your projects.
Navigate to the Timesheets app to start managing your time entries.
Click on the 'My Timesheets' view to see your logged hours in a grid format.
Observe the grid view displaying your time spent on projects per day. Notice the color-coded daily totals at the bottom.
Click on the 'Week' dropdown to change the period view to 'Day' or 'Month'.
Hover over a cell and click the search icon to view and edit the entry's details.
Click the 'Start' button to begin recording time on a project using the timer.
Type or select a different project in the 'Project' field if needed.
Click 'Stop' to end the timer and automatically log the time to your timesheet.
To manually log time, click on the relevant cell in the grid and enter the time spent.
If the project is not visible, click 'Add a line', select the project, and enter the time spent.
Navigate to the Helpdesk app by clicking on the Helpdesk icon.
Go to Configuration and select Helpdesk Teams to manage or create teams.
Click the New button to start creating a new Helpdesk team.
Enter a name for your new Helpdesk team in the Name field.
Optionally, add a description for the team. Remember, this will be visible on the public website form.
Select the company this team is assigned to from the Company drop-down menu.
Under Visibility, choose who can view this team and its tickets. Select an option that suits your team's needs.
In the Followers section, add users who should be notified about updates to the team's tickets.
Enable Automatic Assignment to automatically assign new tickets to team members.
Select an assignment method: either equal number of tickets or equal number of open tickets.
Add team members who will be assigned tickets, or leave it empty to include all employees with the right access.
Click the Save button to finalize the creation of your new Helpdesk team.
Navigate to the Field Service module to begin managing your field operations.
Click the Create button to start a new field service task.
Enter the name of the task in the Name field.
Select a customer for this task by typing in the Customer field.
Choose the date for the task in the Date field.
Add any necessary products or services to the task by selecting them in the Product field.
Click the Save button to save your new task.
Observe the task list to see your newly created task.
Let's start by navigating to the Planning app to manage your team's schedule.
Click on Configuration and then select Roles to define the roles your resources will perform.
Click the New button to create a new role for your team.
Type the name of the role, such as 'Manager' or 'Assistant', in the Name field.
Select the resources that will perform this role, which can be employees or materials.
Now, let's configure the working hours for an employee. Navigate to Employees under Configuration.
Choose an employee to edit their work information and click on their profile.
Go to the Work Information tab to adjust the employee's schedule and roles.
Click on the Working Hours field to set specific working hours for the employee.
Finally, let's observe how roles impact shift assignments. Note that only shifts matching the employee's roles will be displayed.
Navigate to the Discuss app by clicking on the Discuss icon in the app menu.
Once in the Discuss dashboard, take a moment to observe the Inbox, Starred, and History sections where your messages are organized.
To start a new direct message, click the plus icon next to Direct Messages.
Type the name of the person you want to message in the Start a conversation search bar.
Press Enter to start the direct message conversation.
Hover over a direct message to see options like Add a Reaction, Reply, and Mark as Todo.
Click the Notification Settings icon in the top-right corner to adjust your notification preferences for this conversation.
To start a video call, click the Start a Call icon in the top-right corner of the direct message window.
Observe the different controls available during a meeting, such as Mute, Turn camera on/off, and Share Screen.
Navigate to the Calendar app to get started with scheduling.
Observe the current view of your calendar. You can switch between Day, Week, Month, or Year views using the drop-down menu.
Click the 'Today' button to quickly return to the current day in your calendar view.
To sync your calendar with Google or Outlook, navigate to Configuration and then Settings.
Enter your Client ID and Client Secret to set up synchronization with third-party calendars.
Click the 'Save' button to apply your synchronization settings.
To create a new event, click on the target date in your calendar.
In the New Event pop-up, enter the event title and adjust the start and end dates as needed.
Add any additional attendees and include a Videocall URL if needed.
Click 'Save & Close' to finalize your event, or choose 'More Options' for further configurations.
Navigate to the Knowledge app to begin exploring your business knowledge base.
Click the New Article button in the top-left corner to start creating a new article from scratch.
Type the title of your article in the header section to give it a name.
Click on Untitled in the top bar to automatically name the article after the header.
To create a nested article, hover over an existing article in the sidebar and click the plus icon.
Select Load a Template to use a preconfigured template for your article.
Click the Browse Templates button at the bottom of the sidebar to explore available templates.
Select a template that fits your needs and click Load Template to use it.
To edit an article, select it from the sidebar and use the text editor toolbar to format your content.
Click the More actions icon to add a cover picture to your article.
Choose Add Cover to upload or select a cover image for your article.
Let's start by navigating to the Documents app to manage your files.
Click on the 'All' section in the tree to view all folders and files you have access to.
Now, let's create a new folder. Click the 'New' button and select 'Folder'.
In the pop-up, type a name for your new folder and click 'Save'.
Select a folder to open it and manage its contents.
Click on a file to open it and explore the available actions.
To move a file, simply drag and drop it into another folder.
Use the search bar to quickly find specific files or folders.
To configure settings like Deletion Delay, go to Documents Configuration Settings.
Adjust the 'Deletion delay (days)' field to change how long items stay in the trash.
Navigate to the Sign app by clicking on the Sign icon in the app menu.
Click the Create button to start preparing a new document for signing.
Type the name of the document you want to prepare in the Document Name field.
Drag and drop fields onto your document to indicate where signatures are needed.
Click the Save button to save your prepared document.
To send the document for signing, click the Send button.
Observe how you can track the status of your sent documents in the list view.
Click on a document from the list to view its details and status.
If needed, configure advanced settings for signers by clicking on the Settings tab.
Navigate to the Documents app to access the Spreadsheet module.
Click the Create button to start a new spreadsheet.
Type a name for your spreadsheet in the Name field.
Observe how you can insert and link to Odoo data directly within your spreadsheet.
Use the command palette by pressing Ctrl + K (or Command + K on Mac) to explore available spreadsheet commands.
Click on the Insert menu to add a dynamic pivot table to your spreadsheet.
Navigate to the Charts option to visualize your data effectively.
Click on the Share button to share your spreadsheet with colleagues.
Observe how you can use global filters to filter data across multiple Odoo data sources.
Let's start by creating a new to-do. Click the New button or the plus button next to a stage name.
Type a title for your to-do in the provided field.
Click the Add button to save your new to-do.
To add more details, click Edit on your to-do.
In the editor, type '/' to explore formatting options or add media and links.
To convert a to-do into a project task, click the gear button and select Convert to Task.
Choose a Project, Assignees, and Tags, then click Convert to Task to finalize.
To manage your to-do pipeline, drag and drop a to-do to move it to a different stage.
Click the clock button to schedule an activity for your to-do.
Select an Activity Type, Due date, and Assignee, then click Schedule to complete.
Navigate to the Appointments app to begin configuring your appointment settings.
Click on Configuration, then Resources to manage or create new resources for appointments.
Click the New button to create a new resource.
Enter a name for your new resource in the Name field.
Specify the maximum capacity for this resource in the Capacity field.
Set the timezone for this resource to ensure accurate scheduling.
Optionally, select linked resources to handle greater demand if needed.
Add a description for this resource to provide customers with more information.
Click the Save button to finalize the creation of your new resource.
Now, let's configure an appointment type. Go back to the Appointments dashboard and click New.
Enter a title for the appointment type and set its duration.
Define the pre-booking time to ensure appointments are booked in advance.
Navigate to the Dashboards app to start exploring your business metrics.
Click on a dashboard name from the left panel to open it and view the data.
Observe the dashboard layout and the data presented in charts and tables.
Click the double chevron icon to collapse the left panel and maximize your dashboard view.
Use the search bar to apply global filters and refine the data displayed on the dashboard.
Select a conditional operator and a value from the dropdowns to filter the data.
Click the Filter button to apply your selected filters to the dashboard.
To remove a filter, click the remove icon next to the filter name.
Mark this dashboard as a favorite by clicking the star icon in the upper-right corner.
Navigate to the Data Cleaning app to begin managing your data integrity.
Click on the Deduplication section to view potential duplicate records.
Observe the RULE sidebar, which lists active deduplication rules and the number of duplicates detected.
Select a specific deduplication rule to filter and view related duplicate records.
Choose a master record from the group of similar records to merge duplicates into.
Click the Merge button to combine the selected records into the master record.
Confirm the merge by clicking Ok. This will log a message in the master record's chatter.
Navigate to the Configuration section to view and modify deduplication rules.
Click the New button to create a new deduplication rule for a specific model.
Select a model for the new deduplication rule to target and configure additional settings as needed.
Let's explore how AI can assist you in Odoo. Press Ctrl + K to open the command palette.
Type a question or prompt in the command palette and click the AI icon to interact with the Ask AI agent.
Observe the response from the Ask AI agent. Notice the options to send as a message, log as a note, or copy the response.
Hover over the response and click 'Send as Message' to open an email pop-up with the response content.
Now, let's use the AI button. Click the AI button located in the top-right corner of the screen.
In the conversation window that appears, type a request or select a preconfigured prompt to interact with the AI agent.
Notice how the AI agent can assist with tasks like translation, summarization, and text improvement.
To view the conversation in the Discuss app, click on the header of the conversation window and select 'Open in Discuss'.
Navigate to the Discuss app to start integrating WhatsApp with Odoo.
Click the Create button to start setting up a new WhatsApp integration.
In the form that appears, type the name of your WhatsApp Business Account.
Enter the phone number associated with your WhatsApp Business Account.
Click the Save button to save your WhatsApp Business Account details.
Observe the status bar to ensure the WhatsApp integration is active.
Navigate to the Sales app to create a message template for WhatsApp.
Click the Create button to start a new message template.
Type a template name and message content, using placeholders for dynamic data.
Click the Save button to save your message template.
Navigate back to the Discuss app to start a conversation using your new template.
Select a customer and send the message using the template you created.
Navigate to the Phone app by clicking on the Phone icon at the top of your Odoo screen.
Click on the Phone icon to open the Phone pop-up widget, where you can make and receive calls.
Observe the Phone widget. Notice how you can manage calls, send emails, and edit user information directly from here.
To assign VoIP permissions, navigate to the Settings app and select Users & Companies.
Search for a user and open their contact card to adjust their VoIP access rights.
In the Access Rights tab, under the Productivity section, select the desired VoIP access level for the user.
To connect to a VoIP provider, go to the Phone app's Configuration settings.
Click on New to add a new VoIP provider and enter the necessary credentials, such as the websocket URL.
Observe the options for verified VoIP providers like Axivox, DIDWW, and OnSIP, and their configuration details.
If you need to use an alternate provider, ensure they meet the SIP server and WebRTC protocol requirements.
